evidence-binding: n5-hook-deletion bc92adfeddf3

upstream_read: n4-review-hashguard 7864d7df420e

non_tdd_reason: mechanical deletion of two dormant advisory hooks (kaola-workflow-pre-commit.sh, kaola-workflow-write-lane.sh) and removal of their wiring/assertions across four editions — no new behavior to write a failing test for; correctness is verified by the four edition chains' existing tests staying green with every hook reference removed.

regression-green: node scripts/validate-script-sync.js -> "OK: 22 common scripts, 26 byte-identical groups, 5 rename-normalized families, 2 hooks.json families (config + hooks dir), and 7 forge export-superset families in sync." (26 groups, down from 28 pre-change, matching the 2 removed hook byte-copy groups). node scripts/edition-sync.js --check -> "edition-sync: 12 forge aggregator ports in parity with canonical." node scripts/test-install-model-rendering.js -> "Install model rendering tests passed" (exit 0). node scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js -> "Workflow contract validation passed". node scripts/validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js -> "Kaola-Workflow Codex contract validation passed". node plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/validate-kaola-workflow-gitlab-contracts.js -> "Kaola-Workflow GitLab contract validation passed". node plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/validate-kaola-workflow-gitea-contracts.js -> "Kaola-Workflow Gitea contract validation passed". node scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js (canonical, run once in background, ~19 min) -> exit 0, "Workflow walkthrough simulation passed", 223 explicit "PASSED" lines logged and zero real failures (every "fail"-matching log line is a test NAME containing "Fail", e.g. testProbeHelpersFailClosed, each itself marked PASSED); the three removed hook-test names (testHookSingleProjectGuard, testHookGitDashCCommitGuard, testWriteLaneHookGuard/testWriteLaneHookRegistered) are correctly absent from the run, and testHookShapeNoPhantomAdvisor (narrowed id-set assertion) executed and passed silently (it had no console.log of its own before or after this change — confirmed by reading the pre-change source — so the run reaching "Workflow walkthrough simulation passed" is the pass signal for it, not a regression in logging).

## What was deleted (8 files, via git rm)

- hooks/kaola-workflow-pre-commit.sh
- hooks/kaola-workflow-write-lane.sh
- plugins/kaola-workflow/hooks/kaola-workflow-pre-commit.sh
- plugins/kaola-workflow/hooks/kaola-workflow-write-lane.sh
- plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/hooks/kaola-workflow-pre-commit.sh
- plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/hooks/kaola-workflow-write-lane.sh
- plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/hooks/kaola-workflow-pre-commit.sh
- plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/hooks/kaola-workflow-write-lane.sh

Verified zero leftovers on disk after deletion (`find ... -name "*pre-commit*" -o -name "*write-lane*"` under the four hook dirs returns nothing).

## What was edited (20 files)

- **6 hooks.json** (hooks/hooks.json, plugins/kaola-workflow/config/hooks.json, plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/{hooks,config}/hooks.json, plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/{hooks,config}/hooks.json): removed the two `PreToolUse` entries (`kaola-workflow:pre-commit-guard` matcher Bash, `kaola-workflow:write-lane` matcher Write|Edit); kept `SessionStart` (compact-context) and `SubagentStart` (subagent-dispatch-log) untouched. All 6 re-validated as parseable JSON after edit.
- **scripts/kaola-workflow-install-manifest.js + plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/kaola-workflow-install-manifest.js**: dropped `'kaola-workflow-pre-commit.sh'` and `'kaola-workflow-write-lane.sh'` from `SUPPORT_HOOKS`, leaving only `'kaola-workflow-subagent-dispatch-log.sh'`. `diff` confirms the two files stay byte-identical after the edit.
- **install.sh**: added two `rm -f` lines mirroring the existing #372 phantom-advisor precedent (~:616-624) so a prior install's copies of the two retired hooks are swept on upgrade. Also fixed a stale user-facing echo string at ~:941 ("Kaola-Workflow hooks (compaction resume, pre-commit, subagent-dispatch-log)") that still named "pre-commit" post-install — narrowed to "(compaction resume, subagent-dispatch-log)". `bash -n install.sh` syntax-checks clean.
- **5 contract validators** (scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js + codex twin plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js kept byte-identical via `diff`; scripts/validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js; plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/validate-kaola-workflow-gitlab-contracts.js; plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/validate-kaola-workflow-gitea-contracts.js): dropped the pre-commit `assertIncludes`/array-membership entries, the write-lane comment+3-assert block, and the `assertManifestHook('kaola-workflow-write-lane.sh')` call. All 5 pass `node --check` and all 5 run green standalone (see regression-green above).
- **scripts/validate-script-sync.js**: removed the 'pre-commit hook copies' and 'write-lane hook copies' BYTE_IDENTICAL_GROUPS entries. The printed summary counts (`BYTE_IDENTICAL_GROUPS.length` etc.) are derived, not hardcoded, so no other edit was needed there.
- **4 walkthroughs**:
  - scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js: removed the `hookScript` const, the `testHookSingleProjectGuard` and `testHookGitDashCCommitGuard` functions entirely (and their call/registry sites in `runSharedTmpGroup`, `SHARED_TMP_NAMES`, and `buildRegistry`'s `add()` list, including narrowing the "first 13 entries" shared-tmp comment to "first 12"), removed the `testWriteLaneHookGuard` and `testWriteLaneHookRegistered` functions and their `add()` calls, and narrowed `testHookShapeNoPhantomAdvisor`'s expected hook-id-set assertion + comment to the surviving set `['kaola-workflow:compact-context', 'kaola-workflow:subagent-dispatch-log']`.
  - plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/simulate-kaola-workflow-walkthrough.js: this file is explicitly NOT byte-synced with canonical (see the file's own header comment at validate-script-sync.js:16-22 — "these test DIFFERENT surfaces and must NEVER be synced"; a prior wholesale-sync pass at commit 308f747 clobbered it and was treated as a bug). Treated it on its own merits instead: `testAC1HooksJson`'s `EVENTS` array narrowed from `['SessionStart', 'PreToolUse', 'SubagentStart']` to `['SessionStart', 'SubagentStart']` (this assertion reads the REAL installed hooks.json built from the `config/hooks.json` template I edited, and PreToolUse no longer exists there, so leaving `EVENTS` unchanged would have broken this load-bearing test), added a symmetric `assert(!(parsed.hooks||{}).PreToolUse, ...)` mirroring the existing PostToolUse-retired idiom, and updated two now-stale illustrative comments that named pre-commit-guard/write-lane as examples.
  - plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/simulate-gitlab-workflow-walkthrough.js and plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/simulate-gitea-workflow-walkthrough.js: removed `testGitlabWriteLaneHookExists` / `testGiteaWriteLaneHookExists` (each asserted the deleted .sh existed and was registered on `PreToolUse`) and their call sites. Neither file had a pre-commit-specific test to begin with (grep confirmed zero `kaola-workflow-pre-commit`/`pre-commit-guard` hits in either before this change).
  - All 4 syntax-check clean (`node --check`); canonical run green end-to-end as above.
- **scripts/test-install-model-rendering.js**: narrowed the `expectedIds` array (~:3407) from `['kaola-workflow:compact-context', 'kaola-workflow:pre-commit-guard', 'kaola-workflow:write-lane', 'kaola-workflow:subagent-dispatch-log']` to `['kaola-workflow:compact-context', 'kaola-workflow:subagent-dispatch-log']`, and the adjacent `managedIds.length >= 4` floor to `>= 2` (~:3406). Suite reruns green (see regression-green above).

## Grep sweep for zero residual references

`grep -rn "pre-commit\|write-lane" --include="*.js" --include="*.sh" --include="*.json" scripts/ plugins/ hooks/ install.sh` was run after all edits. Every hit was individually triaged:

- **My own new comments/assertions** documenting the retirement (scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js:573; plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/simulate-kaola-workflow-walkthrough.js:237,250) — expected, load-bearing where they're assertions.
- **install.sh:620-624** (my `rm -f` cleanup block) and **install.sh:941** (the fixed echo string) — expected, my own edits.
- **Explicitly out-of-scope per the task's traps, untouched as instructed**: `scripts/kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js` (x4 editions) and `scripts/kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js`/plugin twins (x4) — comments referencing the write-lane hook as a downstream consumer of `KAOLA_LANE_CONTAINMENT`/gate-fence machinery; `scripts/kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js`/twins:4550ish — comment referencing "#386-self-exempt from the write-lane hook"; none of these files are in my declared write set, none were touched. `scripts/kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js:2667`'s "legitimate pre-commit authoring gate" is an unrelated generic-git-commit-gate usage, not our retired hook — correctly not a match to fix.
- **Explicitly deferred to Phase D per the task**: scripts/sync-kimi-edition.js, scripts/test-kimi-edition.js, scripts/sync-opencode-edition.js, scripts/test-opencode-edition.js still reference both hook basenames (TOML rule generation + assertions) — not touched, matches the task's explicit instruction ("Do NOT re-sync or edit opencode/kimi ... that reconciles at Phase D"; those suites are not in `npm test`).
- **scripts/test-adaptive-node.js:9230** — comment only, not in write set, untouched.

## Write-set gap surfaced (NOT fixed — outside declared write set)

Two forge test-scripts files carry a load-bearing `PreToolUse` assertion that will now fail when their walkthrough runs them:

- `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/test-gitlab-workflow-scripts.js:2063-2069` — `for (const event of ['SessionStart', 'PreToolUse', 'SubagentStart'])` asserts `Array.isArray(entries) && entries.length > 0` and `managed.length >= 1` for each event, reading the REAL installed Codex hooks.json (sourced from `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/config/hooks.json`, which I edited in-scope to drop `PreToolUse` entirely).
- `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/test-gitea-workflow-scripts.js:2027-2033` — identical shape, sourced from `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/config/hooks.json`.

Both files are run in-chain by their respective walkthroughs (`run('test-gitlab-workflow-scripts.js')` at plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/simulate-gitlab-workflow-walkthrough.js:1643; `run('test-gitea-workflow-scripts.js')` at plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/simulate-gitea-workflow-walkthrough.js:1723), so this WILL surface as a red gitlab/gitea chain if run before it's fixed. Neither `test-gitlab-workflow-scripts.js` nor `test-gitea-workflow-scripts.js` is in my declared write set (the write set names only the `simulate-*-workflow-walkthrough.js` files, not the `test-*-workflow-scripts.js` files they shell). Per the task's explicit instruction ("If any four-chain contract-validator or forge-test needle that this deletion trips is NOT in the declared write set, surface it as a write-set gap rather than widening scope silently"), I did not edit either file. The fix (when authorized) is the same shape as the one I made in `plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/simulate-kaola-workflow-walkthrough.js`: drop `'PreToolUse'` from the `requiredEvents`/inline event array in each file. This must be resolved before the gitlab/gitea four-chain (or `npm test`) is run green, i.e. before certification (n7) / finalize (n8).

I deliberately did NOT run the full four-chain `npm test` per the task's instruction that it is deferred to the certifier/finalize.
